# Message Loupe

> A free, browser-based second opinion on suspicious email, including business email compromise, wire fraud, invoice redirection, credential traps, and sender impersonation. Drop a saved email or paste raw headers; get a 3-tier verdict (Safe / Caution / Danger) with plain-English reasoning. The email is not uploaded.

Message Loupe is a static web app for non-technical users and small teams who need a second opinion before they send money, change payment details, enter credentials, or open a risky link. The analysis engine is an open-source port of an internal triage tool originally built for phishing analysts, with the analyst-grade pivots stripped out and the verdict reduced to plain language.

## Core promise

- **Privacy.** All analysis runs in the user's browser via JavaScript. There is no server-side processing, no logging, no analytics, no cookies. The user's email is never transmitted anywhere.
- **Limited domain lookups.** For non-webmail senders, optional MX and RDAP requests may send only the visible sender domain to public DNS and registration services. They never include the message, headers, links, or verdict.
- **Honesty.** The verdict is advisory, not a guarantee. The engine can detect impersonation, lookalike domains, authentication failures, and suspicious links, but it is structurally blind to BEC sent from a compromised real account. When the message body mentions money or credentials, the verdict is capped at "Caution: verify by phone."
- **Forwarded-message guard.** A regular forward replaces the original headers with the forwarder's own. Message Loupe detects this and refuses to issue a verdict, asking the user to provide the original instead.

## Pages

- [Home (the scanner)](https://messageloupe.com/): drop a `.eml` file or paste headers; receive a verdict.
- [BEC and wire fraud email checker](https://messageloupe.com/business-email-compromise): direct guidance for wire, invoice, payroll, and account-change emails; explains why authenticated mail can still be fraudulent and cites primary sources.
- [How to save an email](https://messageloupe.com/how-to-save-an-email): step-by-step instructions for Gmail, Outlook web, Apple Mail, Outlook desktop, Thunderbird, iPhone Mail.
- [For teams](https://messageloupe.com/business): small-business workflow and Gmail / Outlook add-in roadmap.
- [Methodology](https://messageloupe.com/methodology): what signals are checked, how the verdict is computed, and the deliberate limits of header-only analysis.
- [About](https://messageloupe.com/about): why the project exists and how it's built.
- [Privacy](https://messageloupe.com/privacy): the no-data-collection policy in detail.

## Source

Open source on GitHub: https://github.com/s56tv8t2mr-cpu/messageloupe
